original_abstract: Background: Participation and performance trends have been investigated in a single stage Ironman triathlon such as the ‘Ironman Hawaii,’ but not for a multi-stage ultra-triathlon such as the ‘Ultraman Hawaii’ covering a total distance of 515 km. The aims of this study were to analyze (1) changes in participation and performance, (2) sex-related differences in overall and split time performances, and (3) the age of peak performance in Ultraman Hawaii.
humanized_abstract: Context: Data on participation and performance trends in single stage Ironman triathlons such as Ironman Hawaii have been reported, but there is no information available on multi-stage ultra-triathlons, like Ultraman Hawaii, covering 515 km. This study aims to investigate: 1) changes in participation and performance in an ultra-triathlon competition; 2) sex differences in performance for total and split times; and 3) the age at which athletes achieve the highest performance.

document.output: Context: Data on participation and performance trends in single stage Ironman triathlons such as Ironman Hawaii have been reported, but there is no information available on multi-stage ultra-triathlons, like Ultraman Hawaii, covering 515 km. This study aims to investigate: 1) changes in participation and performance in an ultra-triathlon competition; 2) sex differences in performance for total and split times; and 3) the age at which athletes achieve the highest performance.
